values: I had it when I passed through the area. After that, who knows
I set it down somewhere and walked away. I do that more than I'd like to admit
I'm almost certain I left it near the road. Almost
The last time I remember having it was during the journey. It could be anywhere along the route
I put it somewhere safe. So safe I can't remember where
It fell out of my pack and I didn't notice until I was already home
I think it's still where I camped. I hope it's still where I camped
It slipped my mind along with half my belongings during the move
I had it at the crossroads. Or was it before the crossroads. The details are fuzzy
I retraced my steps twice. Either I missed it or someone found it first
The cart jostled over a rough patch and things shifted. I only noticed the absence later
It was in a chest I left open during a rest stop. I'm not proud of that
Somewhere between here and there it went from my possession to nobody's possession
I'm embarrassed to say I don't know when I lost it. Just that I did
I packed in a hurry and something got left behind. Something important, as it turns out
The rain was heavy and I was focused on the road. It must have slipped out
I left it leaning against a wall and forgot about it until the next morning
It's the kind of thing you don't miss until you need it. I need it now
I'm fairly sure it's in the area where I made camp. Fairly
I would have gone back myself but I'm not confident I could find the exact spot
I had it. Then I didn't. The gap between those two facts is embarrassingly large
If you find it where I think it is, I'll be relieved. If not, I have no backup plan
I may have dropped it during the storm. The whole trip was a mess
It should still be there. Unless something got to it. Or someone
I set it down to deal with something else and never picked it back up
I know roughly where it should be. Roughly is the best I can offer
I'm hoping it's still where I left it. I'm also hoping I left it where I think I left it
The journey was chaotic. My inventory management was not my priority
I can describe the spot well enough. Whether the item is still at the spot is another matter
I lost track of it. That's the whole story. Not my finest moment
